name: Gambia
experiences: Bijilo monkey walks and beach downtime
Upriver GTSC and pirogue journey to Janjanbureh
Chimp-viewing boat trips in River Gambia National Park
avoid: June to October is wetter and muddier, so ferry queues, reserve trails, and upriver road timings all get less predictable.
Banjul-Barra ferry delays are common around busy weekends and market days, so keep heritage day trips lightly scheduled.
Cash is still essential for park fees, gelli-gellis, bush taxis, and pirogues, especially once you leave the Senegambia coast.
stopoverNote: Banjul (BJL) is the only practical international gateway; the cleanest loop is the coast first for Kololi, Abuko and the Roots sites, then a GTSC or bush-taxi run upriver to Janjanbureh before returning west.

cities.name: Banjul
cities.notes: Compact capital for Albert Market, ferry action, colonial-era streets and the easiest launch point for Gambian history before moving to the beach strip.

cities.name: Serekunda / Kololi
cities.notes: The Senegambia coast base for beaches, restaurants, Bijilo's monkeys, and simple transport links for day trips east or across to Barra.

cities.name: Abuko Nature Reserve
cities.notes: The Gambia's easiest wildlife stop, mixing bird hides, monkey sightings and low-key creek detours within day-trip range of the coast.

cities.name: Janjanbureh (Georgetown)
cities.notes: Sleepy upriver island town for river life, local ferries, roots-era history and the best base for River Gambia National Park boat trips.

itinerary.sim: Africell is the easiest all-round pick for the coast and the long upriver road, with QCell a decent backup; budget roughly ₹360–₹960 for a prepaid SIM bundle with enough data for maps, WhatsApp and ferry lookups.

days.theme: Ease into The Gambia with ferry bustle, market life and a light heritage loop before shifting west to the beach belt.

activities.name: Taxi from Banjul International Airport to central Banjul or Bakau-side hotels
GTSC ferry foot-passenger fare on the Banjul-Barra route
Albert Market browse and produce-stall walk
Arch 22 and Independence Drive walking loop
Local taxi hops between the port, museum quarter and Bakau side

days.theme: Settle onto the Senegambia strip with monkey forest time, a beach reset and the easiest restaurant cluster in the country.

activities.name: Gelli-gelli or shared taxi from Banjul/Bakau into Kololi
Bijilo Forest Park adult entry
Bijilo Forest Park guide share
Kololi beach chair or shade setup
Short taxi hops between Senegambia hotels, Bijilo gate and dinner

days.theme: Use the coast as a launch pad for The Gambia's strongest history day, mixing ferry lines, bush-taxi rhythm and the UNESCO island crossing.

activities.name: Banjul-Barra GTSC ferry round-trip for a self-guided Roots day
Bush taxi or gelli-gelli between Barra and Albreda/Juffureh return
Kunta Kinteh Island UNESCO entry
Albreda Museum and Juffureh museum visits
Pirogue return crossing from the Albreda/Juffureh jetty to Kunta Kinteh Island

days.theme: Keep one slower wildlife day for bird hides, monkey calls and a nearby mangrove detour away from the Senegambia beach strip.

activities.name: Gelli-gelli to Abuko Junction plus final bush taxi hop to the reserve gate
Abuko Nature Reserve adult entry
Reserve guide for birds, reptiles and the bamboo hide
Self-guided reserve trails and observation-hide time
Lamin Lodge creek pirogue or canoe ride after the reserve

days.theme: Trade the coast for an upriver island town, arriving by GTSC and settling into one of The Gambia's slowest, most atmospheric overnights.

activities.name: GTSC Super Express bus from the coast toward Janjanbureh
Local bush taxi from the main road terminal into Janjanbureh town if your bus stops outside the island centre
Short pirogue or ferry crossing if you arrive from the riverbank side
McCarthy Square and riverside walking loop
Market snacks, tea and produce-stop budget

meals: Riverview Restaurant at Island Hotel Janjanbureh

days.theme: Make the upriver leg count with the chimp islands, a longer river boat and one of the best low-volume wildlife days in West Africa.

activities.name: Bush taxi or shared transfer between Janjanbureh and Kuntaur jetty return
River Gambia National Park boat trip including park fee and chimp-viewing permit
Jetty or pirogue landing contribution for the Baboon Islands outing
Riverside birdwatching and town walk after returning from the park
Optional extra local pirogue at sunset around Janjanbureh's banks

meals: Riverview Restaurant at Island Hotel Janjanbureh
